title: NOC 72104 – Structural metal and platework fabricators and fitters | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 72104 professionals are the backbone of Canada’s heavy industrial and construction sectors. As a TEER 2 occupation, these fabricators and fitters are responsible for creating the essential steel skeletons and metal components for skyscrapers, bridges, storage tanks, and massive sea vessels. In 2026, as Canada accelerates its "National Shipbuilding Strategy" and invests billions in green infrastructure like wind turbine towers and modular nuclear housing, these tradespeople are classified as high-priority skilled talent. For immigration, this role is a premier candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and the April 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level2Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group721 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesMinor Group7210 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting tradesNOC Code72104

duties: Blueprint Analysis: Study engineering drawings and blueprints to determine material requirements and plan the most efficient sequence of tasks.
Layout & Marking: Construct patterns and templates; lay out reference points and patterns on heavy metal according to precise component specifications.
Material Handling: Rig, hoist, and move heavy metal sections using overhead cranes and specialized lifting equipment.
Assembly & Fitting: Assemble and fit metal sections and plates to form complete units using tack welding, bolting, or riveting.
Machine Operation: Set up and operate heavy-duty machines like brake presses, shears, and CNC cutting torches to bend, punch, and form heavy components.
Installation: Install fabricated components into the final product, such as bridge spans, ship hulls, or industrial towers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a 3-to-4-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over 4 years of work experience and college-level trade courses.

Certification (Trade Seal): * Trade certification for metal fabricators is available but voluntary in all provinces.Highly recommended to obtain the Red Seal Endorsement, which ensures interprovincial mobility and is a significant advantage for immigration points.
Highly recommended to obtain the Red Seal Endorsement, which ensures interprovincial mobility and is a significant advantage for immigration points.
Proficiency in Blueprint Reading and basic Welding (CWB/GMAW) is often a prerequisite for senior fitter roles.
salary_prospects: Note: Expert platers and shipfitters in the maritime sector or those working in remote oil/gas sites often earn annual salaries exceeding $105,000 due to overtime and site premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections from the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) indicate that while 68% of job openings will come from retirements, the expansion of the Critical Minerals and Nuclear Refurbishment sectors will drive new position growth. The need for specialized platework for hydrogen storage and carbon capture systems ensures long-term demand for fitters through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for structural metal fabricators is Good to Very Good, particularly in industrial and coastal regions. In 2026, Alberta, Ontario, and British Columbia are reporting a steady need for fitters to support the energy, construction, and shipbuilding sectors. While some regions show "Limited" outlooks due to increased automation in standard fabrication, there is a severe shortage of high-precision fitters capable of working on complex, custom-engineered projects like pressure vessels and marine hulls.
Massive Shipbuilding Projects: Work on multi-year federal contracts for Canada’s new combat and non-combat fleet in BC and Nova Scotia.
Green Energy Infrastructure: Lead the fabrication of structural steel for the next generation of wind farms and hydroelectric projects.
High-Tech Tooling: Utilize advanced CNC plasma cutters, robotic welding cells, and 3D laser scanners for precision fitting.
Strategic PR Pathways: This trade is a core component of Express Entry Category-Based Draws for "Trade Occupations," offering a streamlined path to residency.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted under the Trade Occupations category in 2026.
New April 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A one-time measure for 33,000 temporary residents already in Canada, specifically targeting skilled trades.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for shipfitters and metal fabricators supporting the province’s shipbuilding and clean-tech hubs.
Rural Community Immigration Pilot (RCIP): A direct route for fabricators willing to settle in smaller, resource-dependent communities.
